creator: Sohib Boundaoui
body:
Creatives are in Frame.io — ready to post.
→ Download the graphics here

The ads, by name:
  • general_paid-and-sued — broad "paid for years, still got sued?" angle. Runs in CBO or as a broad test.
  • freedom-debt-relief — the $25M CFPB hook. Goes to the Freedom Debt Relief ad set.
  • debtblue — the CROA lawsuits angle ("DebtBlue is in court"). Goes to the DebtBlue ad set.
  • clarity — CROA "fees before service" eligibility. Goes to the Clarity ad set.
  • grt-financial — CROA "fees before service" eligibility. Goes to the GRT Financial ad set.
  • alleviate — CROA "fees before service" eligibility. Goes to the Alleviate ad set.
All 9:16, 10s MP4.

creator: Ads Team
body: @Nacer Amrouch Hi guys as we discussed earlier, our new campaign is starting getting leads and that too in a lower CPC. We are aiming to give this campaign more time so we can scale it gradually.

creator: Ads Team
body:
@Nacer Amrouch ﻿
﻿Salam,

﻿

I wanted to provide a quick update on the Local Services Ads campaign.

﻿

The campaign has generated 752 impressions so far, with a 97.75% Top Impression Rate and a 96.10% Absolute Top Impression Rate, meaning the ads are appearing at the very top of Google search results for the vast majority of eligible searches.


At this stage, no leads have been generated yet. Since this is a new LSA account, it can take some time for the campaign to build performance history and start generating inquiries.

body:
Local Services Ads Performance Report
Reporting Period: 19 June 2026 – 25 June 2026

Top-Level Performance

  • Ad Impressions: 1,854
  • Top Impression Rate on Search: 97.67%
  • Absolute Top Impression Rate on Search: 96.36%
  • Phone Call Leads: 1
  • Total Cost: $0.00

Summary
During this period, the Local Services Ads delivered 1,854 ad impressions with very strong visibility on Search. The ads appeared at the top of search results 97.67% of the time and in the absolute top position 96.36% of the time, which means they were almost always shown in the most prominent spot when they entered the auction.
We also received our first phone call lead during this period. Google has not charged for this lead yet, so the total cost remains $0.00 at this stage.

creator: Sohib Boundaoui
body:
Salam,

New broad targeting campaign for the menopause persona, women only, ages 45 to 60, optimized for the GHL quiz submission. Broad audience with the creative doing the persona sorting, no interest stacks.

  • A1, diet villain. Hook: "The diet that worked at 35 stops working at 45." Answer line: Physician + pharmacist build the one that works now. Creative: 1:1 and 9:16.
  • A1 suggested primary text: The diet that worked in your 30s isn't failing because you quit. Perimenopause changes how your body stores weight, and generic plans don't account for it. At Vous Physique, a physician and clinical pharmacist build a plan around where your body is now. Take the 2-minute quiz.
  • A2, GLP-1 villain. Hook: "A weight-loss shot alone won't fix menopause weight." Answer line: Physician + pharmacist build the plan that does. Creative: 1:1 and 9:16.
  • A2 suggested primary text: A weight-loss shot can help, but on its own, with no plan for what menopause did to your metabolism, the weight comes back. We are not a shot mill: a physician and clinical pharmacist build the full plan around you. Take the 2-minute quiz.
  • A3, dismissive doctor villain. Hook: "It's just menopause" isn't a weight plan. Answer line: Physician + pharmacist build the real one. Creative: 1:1 and 9:16.
  • A3 suggested primary text: Told your weight gain is just menopause and sent on your way? It's real, and it responds to a real plan, built by a physician and clinical pharmacist. Take the 2-minute quiz to start.
  • Watch the 55 to 64 band. It historically clicks but does not convert. Cap at about 58 if it underperforms.
  • Primary text is a starting point only, adjust freely before launch.

creator: Sohib Boundaoui
body:
Salam,

New broad targeting campaign for the PCOS and postpartum persona, women only, ages 25 to 44, optimized for the GHL quiz submission. Broad audience with the creative doing the persona sorting, no interest stacks.

  • B1, postpartum, diet villain. Hook: "Postpartum weight isn't a discipline problem." Answer line: Physician + pharmacist build the real plan. Creative: 1:1 and 9:16.
  • B1 suggested primary text: Postpartum weight isn't a willpower problem, and a calorie app can't see what your hormones are doing after a baby. You deserve a plan built for postpartum, not another bootcamp, designed by a physician and clinical pharmacist. Take the 2-minute quiz.
  • B2, PCOS, dismissive doctor villain. Hook: "Just lose weight" isn't a PCOS plan. Answer line: Physician + pharmacist build the real one. Creative: 1:1 and 9:16.
  • B2 suggested primary text: A PCOS diagnosis and "just lose weight," with no plan for how, isn't care. PCOS changes how your body handles weight and needs a plan that accounts for it, built by a physician and clinical pharmacist. Take the 2-minute quiz.
  • B3, PCOS, GLP-1 villain. Hook: "A weight-loss shot alone won't fix PCOS weight." Answer line: Physician + pharmacist build the plan that does. Creative: 1:1 and 9:16.
  • B3 suggested primary text: A GLP-1 can help with PCOS weight, but without a plan around your hormones the results don't last. A physician and clinical pharmacist build the full plan, with progress you can see. Take the 2-minute quiz.
  • Primary text is a starting point only, adjust freely before launch.

creator: Ads Team
body:
Meta Ads Performance Report
Reporting Period: 12 June 2026 – 18 June 2026

During this period, the campaigns spent $172.02 and generated 1 lead. The campaigns were mainly focused on awareness and retargeting for Clinics, Trade, and Law Firms.

Overall Performance

  • Spend: $172.02
  • Impressions: 3,051
  • Reach: 2,470
  • Leads: 1
  • Cost per Lead: $66.51

Campaign Breakdown

BWS - Law Firms - Awareness

  • Spend: $54.70
  • Impressions: 991
  • Reach: 814
  • Leads: 0

BWS - Trade - Awareness

  • Spend: $45.66
  • Impressions: 828
  • Reach: 665
  • Leads: 0

BWS - Clinic - Awareness

  • Spend: $66.51
  • Impressions: 1,115
  • Reach: 971
  • Leads: 1
  • Cost per Lead: $66.51

BWS - Clinic - Retargeting

  • Spend: $3.43
  • Impressions: 63
  • Reach: 11
  • Leads: 0

BWS - Trade - Retargeting

  • Spend: $1.72
  • Impressions: 54
  • Reach: 9
  • Leads: 0

Summary

The campaigns generated visibility across the three main verticals, with the Clinic Awareness campaign producing the only lead during this reporting period. The Law Firms and Trade campaigns generated reach and impressions but did not produce leads yet.

Since the current campaigns are mostly focused on awareness and website-based conversions, the next step is to make lead generation easier and reduce friction for users.

Next Step

We are going to launch 3 separate Meta Instant Form campaigns for:

  • Clinics
  • Trade Services
  • Law Firms

These campaigns will use Meta lead forms instead of sending users directly to the website. This should help us capture more leads directly inside Facebook and Instagram, while also giving each vertical its own dedicated campaign structure for better testing and optimization.

creator: Ads Team
body:
Meta Ads Performance Report
Reporting Period: 19 June 2026 – 25 June 2026

﻿

This was a transition week. We moved the campaigns over to the new Instagram account, so most of the previous campaigns were paused partway through the period while the new IG setup went live. For that reason, this report focuses on top-level account performance rather than a campaign-by-campaign breakdown, since the per-campaign numbers reflect a mix of old and new setups mid-migration.

﻿

Top-Level Performance

  • Spend: $247.44
  • Impressions: 4,213
  • Reach: 3,041
  • Link Clicks: 310
  • Link Click-Through Rate: 7.36%
  • Average CPC: $0.80
  • Average CPM: $58.73
  • Landing Page Views: 89
  • Leads: 2

Summary
Engagement held up well through the migration. The account delivered a strong 7.36% link click-through rate and a low cost per link click of $0.80, which shows the audiences are responding to the creative. Most of the spend this week sat in awareness and reach-building campaigns, so lead volume was light at 2 recorded leads, and cost per lead is not a representative figure for this period given the awareness-heavy mix and the mid-week account move.

The new IG campaigns are now live and beginning to gather data, which sets us up for a cleaner, more accurate read on performance in the coming weeks.

﻿

Next Steps

  • Let the new IG campaigns run and stabilize so we can establish a clean performance baseline for next week's report.
  • Rebuild lead volume now that the migration is complete, shifting focus back toward the instant form and conversion campaigns.
  • Monitor delivery and engagement on the new IG account over the next few days to confirm everything is pacing and tracking correctly.

creator: Sohib Boundaoui
body:
Period: June 8–14

Location keywords — need rework
Burr Ridge, Orland Park, Homer Glen, Frankfort keywords are showing "rarely served" or zero impressions. Too narrow to get any traffic. Use the towns as location targeting + broader service keywords instead of exact geo keyword strings.

Two low quality keywords — fix
"hvac company near me" and "ac repair near me" are marked Limited – low quality. Improve ad + landing page relevance to lift the quality score.

Working — leave as is
heating and cooling repair: 7 clicks, 1 conversion
hvac contractors near me: 2 clicks, 1 conversion ($8.56 per conv)
Service keywords are converting. Don't touch these.

creator: Nacer Amrouch
body:
Salam,



  • Produce a comprehensive client-facing report covering Quality HVAC performance from when Flow began with them (April 2026) to now. One-time deliverable, not a recurring report.

  • Report covers three tracks: paid ads (Google Ads, Meta, LSA), organic (GBP, website, SEO), and infrastructure (GHL buildout, automations, tracking setup).

  • Ads Team: pull Google Ads, Meta, and LSA performance data from April 2026 to now. Drop all relevant reports and screenshots into this task update before end of week.

  • Hashir: pull organic metrics, GBP progress, website milestones, and GHL buildout status. Drop into this task update before end of week.

  • Nacer compiles all inputs into the final client-facing report and sends to Aiman and Nader by June 6.

@Nacer Amrouch @Ads Team @Muhammad Hashir Faiz
